data.overview: How M&E subcontractors build up a tender price from extracted quantities. The AI extraction provides the quantities — this section explains what happens next so the AI can structure output to be immediately usable by the pricing team.

first_principles.description: Every item measured and priced individually — material + labour + plant per unit
first_principles.when_used: Final tender submissions, negotiated contracts, high-value projects

operational.description: Estimate by composite operations — e.g. 'install a radiator' includes brackets, connections, valves, BWIC as one rate
operational.when_used: Repeat work, framework contracts, negotiated tenders

approximate_quantities.description: M&E cost per m² GIFA benchmarks or cost per functional unit (£/kW, £/lighting point)
approximate_quantities.when_used: Feasibility, early design stages, bid/no-bid decisions

direct_costs.materials: Supplier quotations or database rates. Include delivery, offloading if not in prelims.
direct_costs.labour: Hours × hourly rate. UK M&E rate (2024): £35–55/hour (employed), London +15–25%.
direct_costs.plant_and_tools: Scaffolding, MEWP hire, specialist tools. Often in prelims.

indirect_costs.preliminaries: Site-specific: supervision, welfare, temporary services, scaffolding, security. Typically 8–15% of direct cost.
indirect_costs.head_office_overheads: Company running costs allocated to projects. Typically 5–10%.
indirect_costs.design_fees: If design and build scope. Typically 3–6% of M&E value.

typical_markup_stack.note: Applied to net direct cost (material + labour + plant)

output_format_for_pricing.rule: The AI extraction should produce output structured so the pricing team can apply rates directly. Each line item needs:
required_fields: Item description (e.g., '54mm copper pipe to BS EN 1057')
output_format_for_pricing.grouping: System → Sub-system → Item type → Individual line items
summary_needed: Total linear metres by pipe/duct size
Total m² of ductwork by gauge
